title: crepidomanes pallidum
intro: Crepidomanes pallidum, also known as the pale creeping fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, which is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Crepidomanes pallidum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in infusions or decoctions, and it can also be made into a poultice or topical cream to apply directly to affected areas.
